title: Impact Of Us Port Fees On Dry Bulk Vessel Economics And Fleet Strategy
section_path: Impact Of Us Port Fees On Dry Bulk Vessel Economics And Fleet Strategy
section_path_text: Impact Of Us Port Fees On Dry Bulk Vessel Economics And Fleet Strategy
summary:
Main
The United States remains a vital hub in the global dry bulk trade, with its ports-especially those along the Gulf Coast, East Coast, and Great Lakes- processing significant volumes of coal, grain, and iron ore. Between 2023 and 2024, total voyage intake rose across all major U.S. Gulf (USG), which grew from approximately 140 million to nearly 150 million metric tons (MT).
